title: Japanese encephalitis virus isolation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cg8l0","text":"The mosquito grinding supernatant and CSF samples were inoculated into Vero cells to finish virus isolation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9hfk6","text":"Mosquito grinding supernatant was sterilized using a filter with a pore size of 0.22 μm.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The filtered sterile mosquito grinding solution was inoculated into 24-well plates grown to 80% monolayer Vero cells, 70 μl of each well was added for virus isolation, and a 3-well blank control was set for each plate.(for CSF samples 100μl of each well was added for virus isolation).
1 ml of MEM medium containing 2% FBS was added to each well for maintenance of growth. Cultivate at 37℃ 5% CO2 and observe under microscope every day whether cytopathic effect (CPE) occurs.
Specimens with no CPE were used for blind transmission for three generations .
creator: Wenjing Liu
